title: 8 U.S.C. Sec. 1641
text: Qualified alien definition used to separate lawful immigration categories in domestic-benefit analysis. Section 2 Defines qualified-alien categories used throughout domestic eligibility analysis. Supports the category-separation rule used in the methodology section.

title: 8 U.S.C. Sec. 1611
text: Federal public-benefit restrictions for non-qualified aliens, including the emergency-medical exception structure. Section 2 Section 7 Section 8 Supports the ineligibility baseline for undocumented immigrants in most federal public-benefit programs. Frames the emergency-medical exception carried into the Medicaid analysis. A-037

title: 8 U.S.C. Sec. 1613
text: Five-year bar for many federal means-tested public benefits used in domestic program scope analysis. Section 2 Supports the methodology distinction between qualified categories and waiting-period limits.

title: GAO Budget Glossary
text: Primary budget-accounting terminology for appropriation, obligation, outlay, and related measure-separation rules. Section 2 Supports the rule that appropriation, obligation, and outlay are different accounting states. Supports the anti-mixing rule used across all totals.

title: CBO Common Budgetary Terms
text: Budgetary terminology used to corroborate measure definitions and keep figure types separated. Section 2 Reinforces the methodology rule against mixing budget authority, obligations, outlays, and estimates.

title: ForeignAssistance.gov financial datasets
text: Primary aggregate dataset for foreign-assistance obligations and disbursements used in Section 3. Section 1 Section 3 Supports the approximately $99.9B FY2023 obligations figure. Supports the approximately $71.9B FY2023 disbursements figure when read alongside agency-published foreign-assistance data. Supports the rule that obligations and disbursements are different measures. A-005

title: State/USAID foreign-assistance budget data
text: Supporting published foreign-assistance data used to corroborate disbursement totals and agency composition. Section 3 Corroborates FY2023 disbursement levels and agency composition in the international-assistance lane. A-005

title: Foreign-aid reporting under the Foreign Aid Transparency and Accountability Act
text: Published reporting lane used to frame FY2024 and to-date later-year obligations in the international-assistance section. Section 3 Supports the use of published assistance-reporting channels as the aggregate record of reference for Section 3. A-005

title: CRS IF11437
text: Context source used to distinguish taxpayer-funded assistance from allied-funded foreign military sales. Section 5 Supports exclusion of foreign-funded sales from taxpayer totals in the military-aid lane. A-017

title: SSA primary noncitizen SSI data
text: Primary data for noncitizen SSI recipient counts and average payments used in Section 9. Section 1 Section 9 Supports the published noncitizen SSI recipient count of 365,714 in December 2021. Supports the computed approximately $2.21B annualized SSI amount on the Dec. 2021 basis. A-028

title: Unpublished U.S.-capture share in international assistance
text: Without a published capture-share breakout, the site can report gross taxpayer assistance (Total A) but cannot compute a defensible Total B for international assistance. Section 3 states that Total B is materially below Total A, but not measurable from the current public record. Program-level reporting that distinguishes value captured by U.S. entities from value reaching non-U.S. recipients. Section 1 Section 3 S-038 S-039 S-040

title: Cross-section harmonization between foreign-assistance and military-aid lanes
text: Section 3 uses single-year obligations while Section 5 uses cumulative multi-year military lanes, so a blended headline would overstate precision. The site preserves separate lanes and explicitly forbids a synthetic grand total. A lawful harmonization method that keeps measure type, period, and duplication risks separated. Section 5 S-043

title: ORR consolidated outlay and recipient/provider split
text: The refugee-resettlement lane is routed through states, voluntary agencies, and providers, but the public record does not cleanly isolate value reaching entrants. Section 6 names the program and the gap, but leaves Total B unresolved. Public ORR outlay reporting that isolates entrant-directed value versus provider or operator capture. Section 1 Section 6

title: SSI point-in-time basis versus annual flow basis
text: The SSI figure is computed from a published December 2021 basis, which is strong for that point in time but not a full annual transactional ledger. Section 9 keeps the figure labeled on its Dec. 2021 basis and avoids overstating it as a harmonized annual program total. A published annual SSI spending breakout for noncitizen recipients or a fully reconciled monthly series. Section 1 Section 9 S-073

title: Federal-only emergency Medicaid share
text: The site can publish the federal-plus-state emergency Medicaid figure used in the locked section, but not the federal-only share needed for a cleaner federal total. The limitation is carried forward explicitly in the Executive Summary and Section 7. A public federal-only emergency Medicaid spending breakout for the same period and population basis. Section 1 Section 7 S-003

title: Gross, not net
text: The web edition preserves the audit rule that figures represent gross money directed toward non-U.S. recipients or non-citizens, not net fiscal impact after taxes. Methodology Section 1 Section 2

title: No blended grand total
text: The platform keeps incompatible bases separate and does not synthesize one headline total from disbursements, obligations, cumulative military lanes, and federal-plus-state figures. Section 1 Section 3 Section 5 Section 14 placeholder

title: No population-share modeling
text: Where a federal program does not publish a citizenship-status spending breakout, the site records the gap instead of allocating dollars from population share. Methodology Section 8 Section 9 Section 13 placeholder

title: Citizen-child exclusion
text: Benefits paid for U.S.-citizen children in mixed-status households remain excluded from non-citizen spending totals. Section 1 Methodology Section 8

title: Recipient separated from economic beneficiary
text: The publication distinguishes the direct recipient of federal spending from the economic beneficiary that ultimately captures value, especially in provider-heavy or contractor-heavy lanes. Section 2 Section 5 Section 6 Section 7

title: Drawdown value separated from replacement cost
text: Military drawdown transfer values are not treated as the same thing as taxpayer replacement appropriations, preventing duplicate counting. Section 2 Section 5

title: Executive Summary
text: known minimum incompatible bases no blended grand total noncitizen SSI emergency Medicaid open questions A-005 A-018 A-028 A-037

title: Definitions and Methodology
text: recipient economic beneficiary appropriation obligation outlay evidence class confidence estimation standard category separation

title: International Assistance
text: ForeignAssistance.gov obligations disbursements Total A Total B A-005

title: Military Aid
text: USAI PDA replacement Section 333 CTEF A-017 drawdown replacement

title: Medicaid / Emergency Medical
text: emergency Medicaid A-037 provider capture federal-only share

title: Food Assistance
text: SNAP no modeled share citizen-child exclusion Section 13
